journal.name: Journal of Neurotrauma
journal.notes: Strong home for TBI mechanism, biomarker, and translational synthesis if the evidence chain is explicit and bounded.

Article-type overlap: original research, review

manual_capture_sources: official author instructions supplied manually on April 12, 2026
Journal of Neurotrauma Transparency, Rigor and Reproducibility Statement for FLUID BIOMARKERS Papers (01/05/22)
Journal of Neurotrauma Transparency, Rigor and Reproducibility Statement for PATHOPHYSIOLOGICAL MECHANISTIC STUDIES Papers (01/05/22)

fluid_biomarker_papers.label: Fluid biomarker papers
fluid_biomarker_papers.definition: Human or other living-organism studies that collect biofluids and evaluate biomarker utility for clinically relevant or pre-clinically informative purposes.
must_address: study registration and analytic-plan disclosure
power and planned versus actual sample size
participant accounting for screened, collected, analyzed, quality-passed, and clinically assessed samples
blinding of biomarker measurement, handling, quality control, and analysis roles
sample timing, collection state, handling conditions, storage, batch assignment, lot bridging, and technical failures
assay validation, replicates, coefficient of variation, lower and upper limits, dilutional linearity, specificity, and spike-recovery
validity and reliability of the clinically relevant assessments paired to the biomarker
statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, effect sizes, confidence intervals, and multiple-comparison management
replication or external validation status
data availability, analytic code availability, biofluid sample availability, and open/public availability plan

pathophysiological_mechanistic_studies.label: Pathophysiological mechanistic studies
pathophysiological_mechanistic_studies.definition: Systematic, controlled in vivo or in vitro experimental manipulations intended to advance fundamental mechanistic knowledge relevant to traumatic brain or spinal cord injury.
must_address: study registration and analytic-plan disclosure
power and planned versus actual sample size
subject accounting for inclusion, manipulation, analysis, interpretability, deaths, and incomplete assessments
randomization method and adequacy assessment
blinding of manipulations, materials handling, analyses, and labeling
experimental-manipulation timing, state, devices, storage, batching, technical failures, and unexpected events
target engagement, dose response, cross validation, specificity controls, lesion or stimulation verification, and positive/negative controls
primary-analysis accuracy, assay characteristics, statistical assumptions, outlier handling, missing-data handling, and multiple-comparison management
replication or external validation status
data availability, analytic code availability, sample availability, and open/public availability plan
critical_for_readiness: randomization or explicit rationale for non-randomization

snapshot.notes: Manual capture from the official Journal of Neurotrauma instructions plus the supplied April 2026 article-type-specific transparency statements for fluid biomarker papers and pathophysiological mechanistic studies. This journal explicitly considers original research, reviews, short communications, and letters to the editor; case reports are not accepted. It accepts format-neutral first submissions but still requires IRB/disclosure elements and a 500-word Transparency, Rigor, and Reproducibility Summary after the conclusion. Operational fit rule: mechanistic biomarker and pathophysiological studies can remain bounded in translational maturity if claims are explicitly limited and the evidence chain, declarations, article-type-specific transparency checklist, and reproducibility summary are complete.

snapshot.notes: Official Frontiers pages show Original Research, Systematic Review/Review, Hypothesis and Theory, and Brief Research Report as accepted article types for this journal family. The author-guidelines page points authors to the article-type word-count limits and recommends full section structure for Original Research. Operational fit rule: keep bounded translation below primary-target readiness.

snapshot.notes: Official Springer instructions (updated February 2025) list Original work, Brief communication, Review article, Viewpoint, Editorial, Ethical Matters, Take a closer look at trials, Take Notice: Technology, Neuro-Images, Letters, Invited commentary, and Special article. Original work requires a structured abstract and full Methods/Results/Discussion structure. Operational fit rule: keep bounded translation below primary-target readiness.
